purpose: define the stitched-chain ranking and budget schema on top of the existing bounded continuation/stitching model

problem_statement: post-G12 stitched continuation now exposes multiple chain candidates inside already-selected bounded scope
current ranking is still too file-local and lane-first, so return-looking candidates can beat better alias-result closers
same-path suppression does not eliminate semantically duplicate stitched chains that differ only by path/locality
current bridge-execution provenance is closer to an observed stitched-step union than a true winning-chain explanation

goal: introduce a stitched-chain representative schema above file-local candidate scoring
define closure-first ranking for bounded stitched continuation
define same-chain duplicate suppression and chain-level prune vocabulary
define per-anchor, per-family, and per-seed stitched-chain budgets
split winning-chain provenance from observed supporting stitched steps
non_goal: full argument-binding generalization in this task alone
replacing existing file-level structural guardrails immediately
UI or reporter-focused redesign without ranking/budget changes

core_model.selected_scope_precondition: stitched-chain candidates are reconstructed only from already-selected bounded files/symbols
principles: compare chain representatives rather than raw file candidates when deciding stitched winners
prefer actual closure quality before local lane/evidence heuristics
keep bounded file budgets as structural guardrails while layering chain budgets above them
report the winning chain first and treat other observed stitched steps as support/alternates

evidence.note: these extend rather than replace the current file-local evidence model

origin_reasons.note: links existing file-level reasons to chain-level ranking/provenance

highest_to_lowest: reaches caller_result with relevant stitched closure
reaches wrapper_result or nested_result but caller_result remains support-only
stitched steps are visible but closure_target_kind is supporting_only
bonus_signals: alias-result continuity across wrapper and caller localities
Ruby mixed chain where require_relative plus alias-result stitch actually forms the closing route

signals: non-closing alias chain presented as a closer

duplicate_penalty_rank.rule: weaker chains sharing the same duplicate_chain_key must lose before cross-family final selection

family_fit_rank.purpose: measure whether the coarse family label matches the actual stitched closure instead of just reflecting visible steps on the selected path
examples: penalize return_continuation when alias-result stitching is the actual closing route
penalize mixed_require_relative_alias_stitch when require_relative is only a support observation
reward nested_multi_input_continuation when the relevant binding subset truly drives the chosen closer

ranking_schema.legacy_evidence_role: existing evidence counts and lexical tiebreak remain, but only after closure/penalty/family-fit comparisons

judgement_taxonomy.relation_to_existing_file_prunes: file-level prune reasons remain authoritative for bounded scope; chain-level prune reasons explain why a selected-or-reconstructable chain did not become the stitched representative

budget_intent: prevent thin same-family/same-chain variations from consuming the bounded seed budget before actual chain comparison happens
allow return and alias-result families to coexist long enough for chain-first ranking to compare them
keep existing file budgets as structural guardrails rather than replacing them outright

schema_value: chain representative compare before final compression

directly_targets_failure_families.case: nested multi-input path with noisy alternate

fixed_decisions: ranking, budget, and provenance should pivot from raw file candidates toward stitched chain representatives
closure quality outranks lane-first local heuristics in stitched-chain selection
same-chain duplicate suppression must exist in addition to same-path duplicate suppression
budgeting should distinguish per-anchor, per-family, and per-seed stitched representatives
return and alias-result families must be allowed to coexist long enough for chain-first comparison
provenance must distinguish the winning chain from merely observed supporting stitched steps
existing file budgets remain structural guardrails while chain budgets operate above them
